title: Single-cell rna sequencing using click-chemistry
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a method of sequencing nascent RNA in a cell. In some embodiments, the nascent RNA is conjugated to DNA using copper-catalyzed azide- alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC). Methods of the present disclosure can be used to generate genomic libraries of a cell and measure gene expression and enhancer and/or super-enhancer activity.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is:  CLAIMS 1. A method for analyzing nascent RNA comprising: labeling nascent RNA in one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei by incubating the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with either alkyne-NTPs or azide-NTPs, which are incorporated into nascent RNA transcripts by RNA polymerases in the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei; providing bead-bound azide-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors if the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei are incubated with alkyne-NTPs, or providing bead -bound alkyne-labeled singe-cell barcode-adaptors if the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei are incubated with the azide-NTPs; contacting the lysate of the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with the bead- bound azide-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptor or the bead-bound alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptor in the presence of a copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) reaction reagent, forming RNA-single-cell-barcoded-adaptor conjugates; and optionally, sequencing the nascent RNA or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 2. A method for analyzing nascent RNA comprising: labeling nascent RNA in one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei by incubating the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with either alkyne-NTPs or azide-NTPs, which are incorporated into nascent RNA transcripts by RNA polymerases in the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei; providing free azide-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors if the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei are incubated with alkyne-NTPs, or providing free alkyne- labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors if the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei are incubated with the azide-NTPs; contacting the lysate of the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with the free azide-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors or the free alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode- adaptors in the presence of a copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) reaction reagent, forming RNA-single-cell-barcoded-adaptor conjugates; and 

 optionally, sequencing the nascent RNA or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the azide group is at or near the 5â-end of the bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors or the free single-cell barcode-adaptors. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the sequencing comprises annealing a reverse transcription primer to the bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors or free single-cell barcode-adaptors, and contacting the annealed reverse transcription primer with a polymerase to reverse transcribe the nascent RNA through an adduct formed by the CuAAC reaction. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the adduct is a triazole ring. 6. The method of claim 4 or 5, further comprising incorporating a second adaptor, thereby forming a single-cell barcode-adaptor hybrid. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the incorporation is by a template- switching oligonucleotide or ligation. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a single permeabilized cell or nucleus. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the CuAAC reaction reagent is copper sulfate (CuS04), tetrakis(acetonitrile)copper(I)hexafluorophosphate ((Cu(CH3CN)4]PF6), tetrakis(acetonitrile)copper(I) triflate (Cu(CH3CN)4]OTf, copper acetate (C 4 H 6 Cu0 4 ), copper bromide (BrCu), or copper iodide (Cul). 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein a reducing reagent reduces Cu(II) to Cu(I) in the CuAAC reaction. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the reducing agent is sodium ascorbate, hydrazine, tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP), dithiotreitol (DTT), or beta-mercaptoethanol. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the CuAAC reaction is in the presence of an accelerating ligand. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the accelerating ligand is 2-[4-({bis[(l-tert-butyl- lH-l,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methyl]amino}methyl)-lH-l,2,3-triazol-l-yl]acetic acid (BTTAA), (1- (4-methoxybenzyl)-l-H-l,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methanol (MBHTM), or tris- hydroxypropyltriazolylmethylamine (THPTA) . 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the CuAAC reaction is in the presence of a releasing agent. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the releasing agent is urea. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, further comprising sorting the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei into single wells or encapsulated into aqueous droplets in oil emulsion. 18. A modified rNTP, wherein the ribose or the nucleotide base comprises an alkyne conjugated to: the second carbon of the ribose sugar, the third carbon of the ribose sugar, the sixth carbon of the nitrogenous base, the eighth carbon of the nitrogenous base, the alpha phosphate, the beta phosphate, or the gamma phosphate. 19. The modified rNTP of claim 18, wherein the modified rNTP is rATP, rCTP, rUTP, and/or rGTP. 20. A modified rNTP, wherein the ribose of the nucleotide base comprises an azide conjugated to: the second carbon of the ribose sugar, the third carbon of the ribose sugar, the 

 sixth carbon of the nitrogenous base, the eighth carbon of the nitrogenous base, the alpha phosphate, the beta phosphate, or the gamma phosphate. 21. The modified rNTP of claim 20, wherein the modified rNTP is rATP, rCTP, rUTP, and/or rGTP. 22. A plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors comprising: i) oligonucleotides bound to beads; and ii) an azide group conjugated at or near the 5â end of the oligonucleotides. 23. The plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 22, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the beads comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 24. The plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 22, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the beads do not comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 25. A plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors comprising: i) oligonucleotides bound to beads; and ii) an alkyne conjugated at or near the 5â end of the oligonucleotides. 26. The plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 25, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the beads comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 27. The plurality of bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 25, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the beads do not comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 28. A plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors comprising: i) oligonucleotides; and ii) an azide group conjugated at or near the 5â end of the oligonucleotides. 29. The plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 28, wherein the oligonucleotides of the free single-cell barcode-adaptors comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 30. The plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 28, wherein the oligonucleotides of the free single-cell barcode adaptors do not comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 31. A plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors comprising: i) oligonucleotides; and ii) an alkyne conjugated at or near the 5â end of the oligonucleotides. 32. The plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 31, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the free single-cell barcode-adaptors comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 33. The plurality of free single-cell barcode-adaptors of claim 31, wherein the oligonucleotides bound to the free single-cell barcode-adaptors do not comprise the same nucleotide sequence. 34. A method for analyzing nascent RNA comprising: labeling nascent RNA in one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei by incubating the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with azide-NTPs, which are incorporated into nascent RNA transcripts by RNA polymerases in the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei; providing bead-bound alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors; contacting a lysate of the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with the bead- bound alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors in the presence of a strain-promoted azide- alkyne cycloaddition (SPAAC) reaction reagent, forming RNA-single-cell-barcoded-adaptor conjugates; and optionally, sequencing the nascent RNA or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 35. A method for analyzing nascent RNA comprising: labeling nascent RNA in one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei by incubating the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with azide-NTPs, which are incorporated into nascent RNA transcripts by RNA polymerases in the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei; providing free alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors; contacting a lysate of the one or more permeabilized cells or nuclei with the free alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors in the presence of a strain-promoted azide-alkyne cycloaddition (SPAAC) reaction reagent, forming single-cell barcode adaptor conjugates; and optionally, sequencing the nascent RNA or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 36. The method of claim 34 or 35, wherein the sequencing comprises annealing a reverse transcription primer to the bead-bound single-cell barcode-adaptors or free single-cell barcode-adaptors, and contacting the annealed reverse transcription primer with a polymerase to reverse transcribe the nascent RNA through an adduct formed by the SPAAC reaction. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the adduct is a triazole ring. 38. The method of any one of claims 34-37, further comprising incorporating a second adaptor, thereby forming a single-cell barcode-adaptor hybrid. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein the incorporation is by a template- switching oligonucleotide or ligation. 40. The method of any one of claims 34-39, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a permeabilized single cell or nucleus. 41. The method of any one of claims 34-39, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 42. The method of any one of claims 34-41, wherein the alkyne is bicyclo[6.l.0]nonyne (BCN), /V-[(lR,8S,9s)-bicyclo[6.l.0]non-4-yn-9-ylmethyloxycarbonyl]-l,8-diamino-3,6- dioxaoctane, (lR,8S,9s)-bicyclo[6.l.0]non-4-yn-9-ylmethanol, or [(lR,8S,9s)- bicyclo[6.l.0]non-4-yn-9-ylmethyl /V-succinimidyl carbonate. 43. A method for analyzing RNAs in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: oxidizing 3-terminal cis-diol groups of RNA to dialdehyde; conjugating an alkyne to the dialdehyde by reductive amination by contacting the dialdehyde with a primary amine to form an imine intermediate and reducing the imine to amine using a reducing reagent; isolating the RNAs; and optionally, sequencing the RNAs or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the reducing reagent is cyanoborohydride, sodium borohydride, formic acid, or palladium on carbon (PD/c). 45. A method for analyzing RNAs in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: transferring an azide or an alkyne group to the gamma (g) phosphate on the 5â end of the RNAs; isolating the RNAs; and optionally sequencing the RNAs; or measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 46. The method of claim 45, wherein the transferring is catalyzed by polynucleotide kinase. 47. The method of claim 45, wherein the transferring is by ligation or hybridization of a small oligonucleotide containing azide or alkyne. 48. A method for locating enhancers and/or super-enhancers in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising 

 oxidizing 3â-terminal cis-diol groups of RNA in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to dialdehyde; conjugating an alkyne group to the dialdehyde by reductive amination by contacting the dialdehyde with a primary amine to form an imine intermediate and reducing the imine to amine using a reducing reagent; isolating the RNAs; sequencing the isolated RNAs; and aligning the sequenced isolated RNAs to a reference genome, wherein enhancers and/or super-enhancers are located upstream or downstream of transcribed genes. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the reducing reagent is cyanoborohydride, borohydride, formic acid, or palladium on carbon (PD/c). 50. A method for locating enhancers and/or super-enhancers in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: transferring an azide or an alkyne group to the gamma (g) phosphate on the 5â end of RNAs in the permeabilized cell or nucleus; isolating the RNAs; sequencing the isolated RNAs; and aligning the sequenced isolated RNAs to a reference genome , wherein enhancers and/or super-enhancers are located upstream or downstream of transcribed genes. 51. The method of claim 50, wherein the transferring is catalyzed by polynucleotide kinase. 52. The method of claim 50, wherein the transferring is by ligation or hybridization of a small oligonucleotide containing azide or alkyne. 53. A method for measuring enhancer and/or super-enhancer activity in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising oxidizing 3â-terminal cis-diol groups of RNA in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to dialdehyde; 

 conjugating an alkyne group to the dialdehyde by reductive amination by contacting the dialdehyde with a primary amine to form an imine intermediate and reducing the imine to amine using a reducing reagent to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; sequencing the isolated RNAs; and measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 54. The method of claim 53, wherein the reducing reagent is cyanoborohydride, borohydride, formic acid, or palladium on carbon (PD/c). 55. A method for measuring enhancer and/or super-enhancer activity in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: transferring an azide or an alkyne group to the gamma (g) phosphate on the 5â end of RNAs in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; sequencing the isolated RNAs; and measuring the level of RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR). 56. The method of claim 55, wherein the transferring is catalyzed by polynucleotide kinase. 57. The method of claim 55, wherein the transferring is by ligation or hybridization of a small oligonucleotide containing azide or alkyne. 58. The method of any one of claims 53-57, wherein a higher level of the enhancer and/or super-enhancer activity results in increased RNAs from the genes regulated by the enhancer and/or super-enhancer relative to a control. 59. The method of claim of any one of claims 53-57, wherein a lower level of the enhancer and/or super-enhancer activity results in decreased RNAs from the genes regulated by the enhancer and/or super-enhancer relative to a control. 60. A method of generating a library of nascent RNA sequences in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: oxidizing a 3â-terminal cis-diol group of nascent RNAs in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to dialdehyde; conjugating an alkyne group to the dialdehyde by reductive amination by contacting the dialdehyde with a primary amine to form an imine intermediate and reducing the imine to amine using a reducing reagent to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; and conjugating the isolated RNAs to a bead. 61. A library comprising the sequences of nascent genomic RNA in a permeabilized cell or nucleus generated by the method of claim 60. 62. A method of generating a library of nascent RNA sequences in a permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising: transferring an azide or an alkyne group to the gamma (g) phosphate on the 5â end of nascent RNAs in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; and conjugating the isolated RNAs to a bead. 63. A library comprising the sequences of nascent genomic RNA in a permeabilized cell or nucleus generated by the method of claim 62. 64. The method of claim 62, wherein the transferring is catalyzed by polynucleotide kinase. 65. The method of claim 62, wherein the transferring is by ligation or hybridization of a small oligonucleotide containing azide or alkyne. 66. A method of generating a library of nascent RNA sequences in a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei comprising: 

 oxidizing a 3â-terminal cis-diol group of nascent RNAs in the permeabilized cells or nuclei to dialdehyde; conjugating an alkyne group to the dialdehyde by reductive amination by contacting the dialdehyde with a primary amine to form an imine intermediate and reducing the imine to amine using a reducing reagent to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; and conjugating the isolated RNAs to a bead. 67. A library comprising the sequences of nascent genomic RNA in a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei generated by the method of claim 66. 68. A method of generating a library of nascent RNA sequences in a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei comprising: transferring an azide or an alkyne group to the gamma (g) phosphate on the 5â end of the RNAs in the permeabilized cell or nucleus to form labeled RNAs; isolating the labeled RNAs; and conjugating the isolated RNAs to a bead. 69. A library comprising the sequences of nascent genomic RNA in a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei generated by the method of claim 68. 70. The method of claim 68, wherein the transferring is catalyzed by polynucleotide kinase. 71. The method of claim 68, wherein the transferring is by ligation or hybridization of a small oligonucleotide containing azide or alkyne. 72. A method for sequencing of nascent RNA in a single permeabilized cell or nucleus comprising isolating a single permeabilized cell or nucleus; labeling nascent RNA in the single permeabilized cell or nucleus by incubating the permeabilized cell or nucleus with either alkyne-labeled NTPs or azide-NTPs, which are 

 incorporated into nascent RNA transcripts by RNA polymerase in the single permeabilized cell or nucleus; contacting a lysate of the single permeabilized cell or nucleus with azide-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors or alkyne-labeled single-cell barcode-adaptors in the presence of a copper-catalyzed azide-alkyne cycloaddition (CuAAC) reaction reagent, forming RNA- single-cell-barcoded-adaptor conjugates; fragmenting the nascent RNA by incubating with zinc chloride at 65Â°C; reverse transcribing the nascent RNA into complementary DNA (cDNA); and sequencing the cDNA. 73. The method of claim 72, further comprising amplifying the cDNA by PCR to produce a PCR product. 74. The method of claim 72 or 73, further comprising size-selecting a PCR product by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis. 75. The method of any one of claims 72-74, wherein the single-cell barcode-adaptors are immobilized on a bead. 76. The method of any one of claims 72-74, wherein the single-cell barcode-adaptors are free. 77. The method of any one of claims 72-75, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a plurality of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 78. The method of any one of claims 72-77, wherein the CuAAC reaction reagent is copper sulfate (CuS04), tetrakis(acetonitrile)copper(I)hexafluorophosphate ((Cu(CH3CN)4]PF6), tetrakis(acetonitrile)copper(I) triflate (Cu(CH3CN)4]OTf, copper acetate (C 4 H 6 Cu0 4 ), copper bromide (BrCu), or copper iodide (Cul). 79. The method of any one of claims 72-78, wherein a reducing reagent reduces Cu(II) to Cu(I) in the CuAAC reaction. 80. The method of claim 79, wherein the reducing agent is sodium ascorbate, hydrazine, tris(2-carboxyethyl)phosphine (TCEP), dithiotreitol (DTT), or beta-mercaptoethanol. 81. The method of any one of claims 72-80, wherein the CuAAC reaction is in the presence of an accelerating ligand. 82. The method of claim 81, wherein the accelerating ligand is 2-[4-({bis[(l-tert-butyl- lH-l,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methyl]amino}methyl)-lH-l,2,3-triazol-l-yl]acetic acid (BTTAA), (1- (4-methoxybenzyl)-l-H-l,2,3-triazol-4-yl)methanol (MBHTM), or tris- hydroxypropyltriazolylmethylamine (THPTA) . 83. A method of diagnosing a disease or disorder comprising: analyzing the nascent RNAs from a single permeabilized cell or nucleus according to the methods of any one of claims 1-17, 34-47, or 72-82; and a) sequencing the isolated nascent RNAs; or b) measuring the level of isolated nascent RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR); and comparing the sequence or level of isolated nascent RNAs to control RNA samples to diagnose a disease or disorder. 84. The method of claim 83, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a population of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 85. The method of claim 82 or 83, wherein the control RNA samples are from a known healthy subject not having a disease or disorder, from a subject known to have a disease or disorder, or from a reference sequence, wherein the reference sequence is known to be associated with a disease or disorder. 86. A method of identifying a cell type or types comprising: analyzing the nascent RNAs from a single permeabilized cell or nucleus according to the methods of any one of claims 1-17, 34-47, or 72-82; and 

 a) sequencing the isolated nascent RNAs; or b) measuring the level of isolated nascent RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR); and comparing the sequence or level of isolated nascent RNAs to control RNA samples to identify the cell type or types. 87. The method of claim 86, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a population of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 88. The method of claim 86 or 87, wherein the control RNA samples are from a subject having known specific cell types, from a subject known not to have specific cell types, or from a reference sequence, wherein the reference sequence is known to be associated with a particular cell type. 89. A method of identifying the differentiation state of a cell comprising: analyzing the nascent RNAs from a single permeabilized cell or nucleus according to the methods of any one of claims 1-17, 34-47, or 72-82; and a) sequencing the isolated nascent RNAs; or b) measuring the level of isolated nascent RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR); and comparing the sequence or level of isolated nascent RNAs to control RNA samples to identify the differentiation state of the cell. 90. The method of claim 89, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a population of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 91. The method of claim 89 or 90, wherein the control RNA samples are from a subject known to have a specific differentiation state, from a subject known to not have a specific differentiation state, or from a reference sequence, wherein the reference sequence is known to be associated with a specific differentiation state. 92. A method of identifying the activation state of a cell comprising: 

 analyzing the nascent RNAs from a single permeabilized cell or nucleus according to the methods of any one of claims 1-17, 34-47, or 72-82; and a) sequencing the isolated nascent RNAs; or b) measuring the level of isolated nascent RNAs by quantitative PCR (qPCR); and comparing the sequence or level of isolated nascent RNAs to control RNA samples to identify the activation state of the cell. 93. The method of claim 92, wherein the nascent RNA is sequenced from a population of permeabilized cells or nuclei. 94. The method of claim 92 or 93, wherein the control RNA samples are from a subject known to have a specific activation state, from a subject known to not have a specific activation state, or from a reference sequence, wherein the reference sequence is known to be associated with a specific activation state.
